summary: Postinstall script downloads and executes per-OS malware with persistence
details: On `npm install`, package.json's postinstall hook runs install.js, which selects a base64-encoded shell command per platform and executes it via child_process.exec. The decoded payloads fetch platform-specific binaries from https://www.mythicalsgames.com/files/sean/ and establish persistence: on Windows, SvcHostUpdate.exe is downloaded and dropped into the user's Startup folder; on Linux, syslog-service.py is written to ~/.local/share/.syslog with an @reboot crontab entry; on macOS, a com.microsoft.VSCodeUpdate-darwin binary is fetched, has its quarantine attribute stripped, and is executed. install.js carries a deliberate cover-story comment (`// Safe no-op installer`) and prints `postinstall script running (no-op)` to mask the real behavior. The package's advertised purpose (`Utilities for Web3 token applications`) is unrelated to anything the install script does, indicating a lure package targeting Web3/ethers developers.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
